description: Security skill should produce structured quality findings, not generic responses. Tests Opus judge integration.
input_prompt: audit: check the codebase for security issues
expected_behavior: Claude invokes the security audit skill and presents findings with rule IDs, severity levels, and specific file locations. Response is structured, not generic.
negative_checks: Claude does not say 'I cannot audit code' or refuse the request
Claude does not provide findings without specific file paths or line numbers
fixture_transcript:
User: audit: check the codebase for security issues

Claude: Running security audit on the codebase.

[Invoked: ds-quality:security]

Security Audit Results:

🔴 sec-002 [CRITICAL] SQL Injection — core/analytics/aggregate_metrics.py:147
  Found: f-string with SQL keywords and variable interpolation
  Fix: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute(sql, (param,))

⚠️ sec-013 [HIGH] PII in logs — projections/api/routes/shared_intelligence.py:203  
  Found: logger.info() with email variable
  Fix: Log opaque user_id instead of email

✓ 2 critical/high findings. Run ds-quality security audit for full report.
